Sub-Category Name: Benefit activated late requiring backdated correction
Parent Category: Benefit activation, backdating, or late setup
Definition: A benefit was not activated at the correct start date, requiring a one-off adjustment to recover missed months

Sub-Category Name: Benefit activated for wrong employee or coverage
Parent Category: Benefit activation, backdating, or late setup
Definition: A benefit was activated under the wrong employee profile, dependant tier, or coverage type and requires correction

Sub-Category Name: Prior period benefit correction
Parent Category: Benefit activation, backdating, or late setup
Definition: A correction to a benefit amount or configuration that should have been applied in a prior period, processed in the current month

Sub-Category Name: Benefit amount calculated at wrong rate or value
Definition: A benefit such as pension, medical, or mutuelle is active but calculated using the wrong rate, percentage, or fixed amount

Sub-Category Name: Benefit configured with incorrect frequency or type
Definition: A benefit has been set up with the wrong frequency (e.g. annual charged monthly) or wrong classification, requiring a refund or correction

Sub-Category Name: Meal voucher amount differs from benefits team confirmation
Definition: The meal voucher amount processed in payroll differs from the amount confirmed by the benefits team, requiring alignment

Sub-Category Name: Benefit grossed up incorrectly or gross-up removed
Definition: An employer benefit allowance was grossed up when it should not be, or stopped being grossed up, changing the net amount due

Sub-Category Name: Benefit processed by provider but not invoiced by Playroll
Parent Category: Benefit not invoiced or missing from report
Definition: A benefit payment was made by the provider or confirmed correct but was omitted from the Playroll invoice and must be recovered

Sub-Category Name: Benefit accrual or contribution missing from report
Parent Category: Benefit not invoiced or missing from report
Definition: An employer benefit contribution or accrual was not included in the payroll report due to a processing or reporting gap

Sub-Category Name: Benefit overcharged — refund due to client
Definition: A benefit was invoiced at a higher amount than correctly due, or charged in error, and a credit must be issued to the client

Sub-Category Name: Benefit charged after termination
Definition: A benefit continued to be processed or invoiced after an employee's termination date and must be refunded

Sub-Category Name: Benefit not yet activated — charge reversed
Definition: A benefit was invoiced before it was formally activated or approved, requiring a reversal of the charge

Sub-Category Name: Salary apportionment method incorrectly programmed
Definition: The calculator uses the wrong day basis (e.g. working days vs calendar days) to prorate salary for a starter, leaver, or partial month

Sub-Category Name: Contribution cap apportionment error
Definition: Contribution caps are apportioned using an incorrect formula producing an overstated or understated cap

Sub-Category Name: Allowance or benefit apportionment error
Definition: An allowance or benefit is prorated using the wrong method or not prorated when it should be

Sub-Category Name: Pension contribution incorrectly programmed
Definition: The employer or employee pension contribution is calculated at the wrong rate, on the wrong base, or excluded entirely due to a programming error

Sub-Category Name: Health or insurance contribution programming error
Definition: An employer health or insurance contribution is missing, applied at the wrong rate, or not accounting for the employee's specific fund or plan

Sub-Category Name: Benefit contribution omitted due to code change
Definition: A benefit contribution that was correctly programmed was subsequently removed or broken by a platform code change

Sub-Category Name: Contribution base incorrectly configured
Definition: The earnings base used to calculate a statutory contribution is wrong — either including the wrong components or missing required ones

Sub-Category Name: Accrual or contribution not invoiced due to programming omission
Definition: A mandatory accrual or contribution is confirmed correct but was omitted from the invoice due to a programming error or omission

Sub-Category Name: Contribution double-counted or applied twice
Definition: The calculator applies the same contribution twice simultaneously due to a logic error — conditions that should be mutually exclusive are both active

Sub-Category Name: Accrual rate or percentage incorrectly programmed
Definition: The percentage used to calculate a leave, gratuity, or statutory bonus accrual is incorrectly coded in the calculator

Sub-Category Name: Partner bank or forex charge not programmed
Parent Category: Partner or territory-specific charge not programmed
Definition: A recurring bank commission or foreign exchange fee charged by the payroll partner is not captured in the calculator and requires a manual monthly adjustment

Sub-Category Name: Territory-specific statutory charge not programmed
Parent Category: Partner or territory-specific charge not programmed
Definition: A mandatory territory-specific levy, admin cost, or statutory charge is not built into the calculator and recurs as a monthly adjustment

Sub-Category Name: Statutory rate not updated in calculator
Parent Category: Rate, cap, or threshold incorrectly programmed
Definition: The calculator is still using an old statutory contribution or tax rate that has since changed, and the update has not yet been applied

Sub-Category Name: Contribution cap not correctly enforced
Parent Category: Rate, cap, or threshold incorrectly programmed
Definition: The calculator continues charging contributions beyond the statutory cap limit because the cap logic has not been correctly programmed

Sub-Category Name: Incorrect statutory update applied
Parent Category: Rate, cap, or threshold incorrectly programmed
Definition: A platform update was made to correct a rate or cap but contained an error, resulting in a wrong value being applied after the update

Sub-Category Name: Contribution rate programmed at wrong value
Parent Category: Rate, cap, or threshold incorrectly programmed
Definition: The rate applied by the calculator for a specific contribution is confirmed to be incorrect and differs from the statutory or provider-confirmed rate

Sub-Category Name: Complex territory tax logic incorrectly programmed
Parent Category: Complex statutory or tax logic error
Definition: A territory-specific tax rule or interaction between multiple tax rules is programmed incorrectly, producing a systematic error across all employees in that territory

Sub-Category Name: Termination or leave payout tax not programmed
Parent Category: Complex statutory or tax logic error
Definition: Employer contributions or taxes applicable to termination payouts, leave encashment, or backpay are not included in the calculator

Sub-Category Name: Length-of-service or seniority increment not programmed
Parent Category: Complex statutory or tax logic error
Definition: An automatic pay or contribution increase triggered by an employee's service milestone is not captured in the calculator

Sub-Category Name: Leave or holiday accrual at average salary rate
Definition: Leave or holiday accrual uses average prior period earnings including variable pay rather than current salary

Sub-Category Name: Contribution or accrual base includes average earnings
Definition: Employer contributions or statutory accruals need to incorporate average earnings including bonuses or commissions

Sub-Category Name: Statutory bonus accrual (13th / 14th cheque)
Definition: Accrual provision for mandatory bonus payments calculated from prior period average earnings

Sub-Category Name: Accrual rate or percentage error
Definition: Wrong percentage applied to a leave, gratuity, or bonus accrual

Sub-Category Name: Gratuity or long-service accrual
Definition: Ongoing monthly provision for gratuity or long-service entitlement that differs from platform calculation

Sub-Category Name: New starter salary proration
Definition: Salary prorated for partial first month using a different day basis than the calculator

Sub-Category Name: Leaver salary proration
Definition: Salary apportioned to the termination date for the final month

Sub-Category Name: Calendar days vs working days method
Definition: Provider uses calendar day apportionment while the calculator uses working days, or vice versa

Sub-Category Name: Multi-pay-period territory apportionment
Definition: Territory with more than one pay period per month where apportionment is done per period

Sub-Category Name: Allowance or benefit proration
Definition: Allowance, benefit, or contribution prorated for a partial month starter or leaver

Sub-Category Name: Prior month new starter catchup
Definition: Salary or contribution for a new starter processed in the wrong period and corrected in the current month

Sub-Category Name: Wrong earnings base for contributions
Parent Category: Contribution base, rates, and employer charges
Definition: Contributions calculated on an incorrect base that excludes or includes the wrong earnings components

Sub-Category Name: Incorrect contribution rate applied
Parent Category: Contribution base, rates, and employer charges
Definition: Wrong statutory rate applied for pension, social security, or employer charge

Sub-Category Name: Territory-specific contribution not programmed
Parent Category: Contribution base, rates, and employer charges
Definition: Recurring territory-specific charge not built into the calculator (e.g. AR OSDE, AT BV contribution)

Sub-Category Name: Retrospective or backdated contribution
Parent Category: Contribution base, rates, and employer charges
Definition: Contribution applied retroactively or correction for a prior missed or incorrectly processed period

Sub-Category Name: Social security refund or reimbursement
Parent Category: Contribution base, rates, and employer charges
Definition: Government SS refund received for maternity or sick leave, reducing employer cost

Sub-Category Name: Employee-specific contribution arrangement
Parent Category: Contribution base, rates, and employer charges
Definition: Split forms, individual agreements, study fund, or non-standard employee contribution structures

Sub-Category Name: Multi-rule statutory interaction
Definition: Calculation requires simultaneous application of multiple interacting statutory rules with no simpler single-rule equivalent

Sub-Category Name: Mandatory periodic statutory payment formula
Definition: Legally required recurring payment with a formal territory-specific calculation formula (e.g. CL legal gratification)

Sub-Category Name: Seniority or length-of-service triggered adjustment
Definition: Mandatory pay or contribution change triggered when an employee reaches a service milestone

Sub-Category Name: Territory-specific recurring charge with frequent rule changes
Definition: Ongoing territory-specific charge with frequent statutory changes or complex computation not classifiable elsewhere

Sub-Category Name: Annual contribution cap not enforced
Definition: Calculator continues invoicing contributions after the annual statutory limit is reached

Sub-Category Name: Annual cap exceeded due to variable income
Definition: Cap reached earlier because bonuses or commissions are included in insurable earnings but the calculator cannot account for this

Sub-Category Name: Quarterly cap applied as monthly
Definition: Statutory quarterly cap (e.g. AU superannuation) incorrectly applied on a per-month basis

Sub-Category Name: Pension waiting period not yet reached
Definition: Employer pension contributions not yet applicable because employee is still in a qualifying waiting period

Sub-Category Name: Cap apportionment for partial-month period
Definition: Contribution cap must be prorated for a starter or leaver month but calculator cannot perform this

Sub-Category Name: Hourly or variable-hours pay
Parent Category: Employee-specific setup and special earning rules
Definition: Employee paid by the hour or by actual hours logged; no calculator functionality to invoice accurately

Sub-Category Name: Employee-specific contribution rate
Parent Category: Employee-specific setup and special earning rules
Definition: Individual contribution rates driven by employee-specific factors (profession, insurance class, personal agreement)

Sub-Category Name: Pension opt-out, sacrifice, or override
Parent Category: Employee-specific setup and special earning rules
Definition: Employee has a non-standard pension arrangement: opted out, salary sacrifice, or a custom contribution override

Sub-Category Name: Overtime and additional hours pay
Parent Category: Employee-specific setup and special earning rules
Definition: Employee worked overtime or public holiday hours requiring a different pay rate

Sub-Category Name: Salary structure or peg rate
Parent Category: Employee-specific setup and special earning rules
Definition: Employee salary uses a peg rate, currency conversion, or split structure that differs from the standard calculation

Sub-Category Name: Mid-period salary change
Parent Category: Employee-specific setup and special earning rules
Definition: Salary change effective mid-month requiring a blended or prorated calculation for the period

Sub-Category Name: Expense reimbursement grossed up
Definition: Employee expenses subject to tax and contributions, requiring a gross-up to arrive at the correct net payment

Sub-Category Name: Net-to-gross salary conversion
Definition: Employee on a net salary arrangement requiring a gross-up calculation the platform cannot perform

Sub-Category Name: Work-from-home or per diem allowance
Definition: WFH payment or per diem for business travel with a specific tax treatment

Sub-Category Name: Gross-up on bonus or one-off payment
Definition: Bonus or additional payment subject to gross-up rather than standard salary tax treatment

Sub-Category Name: Annual leave paid at average rate
Definition: Leave taken and paid at average of prior period earnings rather than current month salary

Sub-Category Name: Sick leave paid at reduced statutory rate
Definition: Sick leave paid at territory-specific reduced rate (70%, 80%, 50% etc.)

Sub-Category Name: Unpaid leave salary deduction
Definition: Salary reduced for days of unpaid, parental, or family leave

Sub-Category Name: Parental and maternity leave pay
Definition: Maternity/paternity leave with employer top-up, state payment, or salary replacement

Sub-Category Name: Public holiday pay at average rate
Definition: Public holidays paid at 12-month average earnings rather than monthly salary

Sub-Category Name: Leave-linked benefit reduction
Definition: Meal vouchers, allowances, or other benefits reduced because of leave days taken

Sub-Category Name: Retrospective leave correction
Definition: Prior period leave processed, cancelled, or corrected after the original payroll run

Sub-Category Name: Foreign exchange and bank charges
Definition: Variance caused by exchange rate differences or bank transfer charges, not a payroll calculation error

Sub-Category Name: Mandatory employer levy or penalty
Definition: Mandatory employer penalty or levy charged by a partner or authority that cannot be programmed in the calculator

Sub-Category Name: Post-OOC or late input processing
Definition: Adjustment arising from a payroll change submitted after cut-off and handled outside the normal process

Sub-Category Name: Termination leave encashment calculation
Parent Category: Termination, one-off corrections, and manual true-ups
Definition: Unused leave days cashed out on termination using a territory-specific method (e.g. India 1/3 basic salary rule)

Sub-Category Name: Termination payout timing and amount
Parent Category: Termination, one-off corrections, and manual true-ups
Definition: Final pay, notice pay, or severance calculated at a different amount or paid in a different period than expected

Sub-Category Name: Tax treatment of termination payment
Parent Category: Termination, one-off corrections, and manual true-ups
Definition: NI, income tax, or social security incorrectly applied or omitted on termination payouts

Sub-Category Name: Gratuity refund or adjustment on termination
Parent Category: Termination, one-off corrections, and manual true-ups
Definition: Gratuity collected refunded because employee has not met qualifying tenure, or gratuity recalculated on exit

Sub-Category Name: Manual true-up or prior period correction
Parent Category: Termination, one-off corrections, and manual true-ups
Definition: Correction for an error or omission in a prior month, not linked to a termination event

Sub-Category Name: Mandatory statutory one-off payout
Parent Category: Termination, one-off corrections, and manual true-ups
Definition: Legally required one-off payments (e.g. BE ecocheques, prime annuelle, DE disability levy) not captured in calculator

Sub-Category Name: Unexplained variance — provider confirmed correct
Definition: Provider calculation confirmed as correct but the specific cause of the discrepancy is not understood

Sub-Category Name: Retrospective correction from statutory rule change
Definition: Adjustment triggered by a change in minimum wage, tax rate, or statutory threshold requiring retroactive recalculation

Sub-Category Name: Outdated rate applied — UTP pending
Definition: Calculator using an old statutory rate where the correct updated rate is known but not yet pushed to the platform

Sub-Category Name: Territory-specific tax ruling or exemption
Parent Category: Tax treatment, exemptions, and territory-specific tax rules
Definition: Country-specific ruling that reduces or eliminates tax liability (e.g. NL 30% ruling, ZA foreign income exemption, BA zero ER contributions)

Sub-Category Name: Taxable vs non-taxable allowance or benefit
Parent Category: Tax treatment, exemptions, and territory-specific tax rules
Definition: Allowances, benefits, or bonuses with a split between taxable and non-taxable treatment

Sub-Category Name: Non-taxable one-off or bonus payment
Parent Category: Tax treatment, exemptions, and territory-specific tax rules
Definition: Bonus or one-off payment exempt from social security or contributions by territory rule

Sub-Category Name: Social security discount or contribution reduction
Parent Category: Tax treatment, exemptions, and territory-specific tax rules
Definition: Employee or employer eligible for a statutory SS reduction (e.g. HU family discount, NL ruling-based SS base reduction)

Sub-Category Name: Incorrect tax treatment of reimbursement
Parent Category: Tax treatment, exemptions, and territory-specific tax rules
Definition: Expense or reimbursement processed as tax-free when it is taxable, or vice versa

Sub-Category Name: Territory-specific statutory charge not programmed
Parent Category: Tax treatment, exemptions, and territory-specific tax rules
Definition: Statutory levy, VAT on benefit, or special charge the calculator cannot model (e.g. CN VAT on office seat)

Sub-Category Name: Client submitted incorrect salary or bonus amount
Parent Category: Incorrect payroll data submitted by client
Definition: The client entered the wrong salary, bonus, or variable pay amount on the platform, requiring correction after the payroll run

Sub-Category Name: Client submitted incorrect or duplicate allowance
Parent Category: Incorrect payroll data submitted by client
Definition: The client added an allowance that was already included in the salary structure, or entered it twice, resulting in a duplicate charge

Sub-Category Name: Client submitted wrong employee for payment
Parent Category: Incorrect payroll data submitted by client
Definition: The client entered a payment or instruction against the wrong employee on the platform

Sub-Category Name: Client notified of change after cut-off
Definition: The client informed Playroll of a salary change, termination, leave, or benefit change after the payroll cut-off, requiring an out-of-cycle correction

Sub-Category Name: Client decision change after payroll processed
Definition: The client changed a payroll decision after the payroll had already been processed, requiring a reversal or adjustment

Sub-Category Name: Private health insurance rate not configured on profile
Definition: The employee's private health insurance contribution rate or fund has not been set up on their profile, causing a variance with the provider

Sub-Category Name: Employee-specific insurance or contribution rate
Definition: The employee has an individual insurance rate, fund, or contribution amount that differs from the standard and requires a profile-level override

Sub-Category Name: Non-standard pension contribution rate or override
Definition: Employee has a pension contribution rate that differs from the default and the profile has not been updated to reflect this

Sub-Category Name: Pension contribution basis incorrectly configured
Definition: The earnings base used to calculate pension contributions is wrong on the employee profile (e.g. applied to gross instead of basic salary)

Sub-Category Name: Backdated pension correction due to profile update
Definition: A pension profile update was made retroactively, requiring corrections for the periods during which the wrong setup was active

Sub-Category Name: Salary incorrectly configured on profile
Definition: The employee's salary amount, structure, or currency is incorrectly set up on their profile, causing a systematic monthly variance

Sub-Category Name: Net-to-gross salary arrangement not configured
Definition: The employee is on a net salary arrangement that requires a gross-up, which is not correctly set up on their profile

Sub-Category Name: Allowance missing or incorrect on profile
Definition: A recurring allowance is missing from the employee's profile or is configured at the wrong amount or frequency

Sub-Category Name: Gratuity basis or rate incorrectly configured
Definition: The gratuity calculation basis (e.g. percentage of salary, eligible salary components) is incorrectly set up on the employee's profile

Sub-Category Name: Mandatory family or dependant allowance not on profile
Definition: A statutory family or dependant allowance the employee is entitled to has not been added to their profile

Sub-Category Name: EOR or territory-specific contribution not configured
Definition: A contribution specific to the EOR entity or territory is missing or incorrectly assigned on the employee profile

Sub-Category Name: Salary, bonus, or allowance changed after cut-off
Definition: A salary, bonus, or allowance was added or changed after the payroll cut-off, requiring an adjustment to align the invoice with the updated payroll

Sub-Category Name: Deduction or leave change submitted after cut-off
Definition: A deduction, leave record, or benefit change was submitted after cut-off and was not captured in the original payroll run

Sub-Category Name: New starter onboarded after cut-off
Definition: An employee was onboarded after the payroll cut-off and their full employment cost must be invoiced via an out-of-cycle adjustment

Sub-Category Name: Employee terminated after cut-off
Definition: An employee's termination was confirmed or processed after the payroll cut-off, requiring a reversal of the invoiced salary and associated payouts

Sub-Category Name: Start date changed after cut-off
Definition: An employee's start date was changed after cut-off, requiring a full or partial refund of the invoiced employment cost for the affected period

Sub-Category Name: Employee did not start — full refund required
Definition: An employee whose cost was invoiced did not start employment, requiring a full refund of the invoiced amount

Sub-Category Name: Accrual adjusted to match provider calculation
Definition: The Playroll accrual differs from the amount the provider has accrued and the invoice is adjusted to match the provider's confirmed figure

Sub-Category Name: Leave payout accrual correction
Definition: The accrual for leave payout is corrected after a leave balance reconciliation or a change in the employee's leave usage or payout basis

Sub-Category Name: Accrual refunded on termination — entitlement not met
Definition: Accrual collected for gratuity, leave, or a statutory bonus is refunded to the client because the employee did not meet the qualifying tenure or conditions

Sub-Category Name: Accrual reversed due to overpayment or cancellation
Definition: An accrual is reversed because it was collected in excess of the actual entitlement or because the underlying obligation no longer applies

Sub-Category Name: 13th or 14th cheque accrual
Definition: Monthly accrual for the statutory 13th or 14th month bonus, adjusted to reflect the correct payroll-calculated entitlement for the period

Sub-Category Name: Christmas or annual statutory bonus accrual
Definition: Monthly accrual for a mandatory Christmas or year-end bonus, requiring adjustment where the accrual rate or base has changed

Sub-Category Name: CTS or long-service statutory accrual
Definition: Accrual for a territory-specific statutory severance or long-service provision where the correct base includes variable earnings

Sub-Category Name: Deduction not applied in payroll
Definition: A required salary deduction was not processed in payroll and must be recovered via an adjustment

Sub-Category Name: Leave or sick pay not reflected in invoice
Definition: Leave taken or sick pay applied by the provider was not reflected in the Playroll invoice

Sub-Category Name: Incorrect salary processed
Definition: The wrong salary amount was processed due to a data entry error, profile issue, or incorrect change made on the platform

Sub-Category Name: Incorrect allowance processed
Definition: An allowance was processed at the wrong amount, duplicated, or included when it should not have been

Sub-Category Name: Incorrect bonus or variable pay processed
Definition: A bonus or variable pay component was processed at the wrong amount or attributed to the wrong employee

Sub-Category Name: Overtime or on-call pay not invoiced
Definition: Overtime or on-call payments were correctly processed in payroll but not included in the Playroll invoice

Sub-Category Name: Bonus or allowance not invoiced
Definition: A bonus or allowance was processed by the provider but missing from the Playroll invoice

Sub-Category Name: Hourly pay not accurately invoiced
Definition: An hourly employee's actual hours were not captured correctly in the invoice due to missing functionality

Sub-Category Name: Quarterly statutory charge invoiced and apportioned
Definition: A statutory charge invoiced quarterly (e.g. HU business tax) is apportioned across employees and processed as a periodic adjustment

Sub-Category Name: Shift differential or special pay rate
Definition: A payment for shift work, unsociable hours, or a special pay rate was not included in the standard invoice and requires adjustment

Sub-Category Name: Termination payout not captured in invoice
Definition: A termination-related payout was processed by the provider but not included in the Playroll invoice

Sub-Category Name: Prior period payroll correction
Definition: A correction for an error or omission in a prior month's payroll that is being processed in the current period

Sub-Category Name: Provider calculated contribution or salary incorrectly
Definition: The provider processed an incorrect salary, contribution, bonus, or tax amount and the error is confirmed and being corrected in the current or following period

Sub-Category Name: Provider omitted or misattributed a payment
Definition: The provider omitted a payment entirely or attributed it to the wrong employee, requiring a correction in the current period

Sub-Category Name: Provider correcting their own prior period error
Definition: The provider is processing a correction in the current month for an error they made in a prior payroll run, generating an adjustment variance

Sub-Category Name: Partner bank or forex charge passed through
Parent Category: Provider charges additional fees or levies
Definition: The payroll partner charges a bank commission or foreign exchange fee that is passed through to the client and not captured in the Playroll cost calculator

Sub-Category Name: Territory-specific levy or subscription charged by partner
Parent Category: Provider charges additional fees or levies
Definition: The provider invoices for a territory-specific charge, fund subscription, or occupational risk fee that is not programmed into the Playroll calculator

Sub-Category Name: Provider uses different exchange or salary rate
Definition: The provider calculates payroll using a different exchange rate or salary peg than Playroll, creating a systematic variance each month

Sub-Category Name: Provider applies different formula for variable pay
Definition: The provider includes commissions, overtime, or variable earnings in a calculation using a method that differs from Playroll

Sub-Category Name: Provider accrual rate or basis differs from Playroll
Definition: The provider accrues for statutory obligations at a different rate or on a different base than Playroll's programmed accrual

Sub-Category Name: 13th or 14th cheque processed on provider's schedule
Definition: The provider pays out the 13th or 14th cheque based on local statutory deadlines that differ from the Playroll invoicing cycle, creating a timing adjustment

Sub-Category Name: Mandatory vacation or holiday bonus paid out by provider
Definition: The provider pays out a legally required vacation or holiday bonus when the statutory trigger occurs, creating a variance against the accrual collected

Sub-Category Name: Leave payout calculated in wrong currency or amount
Definition: The platform calculated the leave payout using the wrong currency, wrong basis, or wrong amount due to a product-level bug

Sub-Category Name: Contribution or deduction double-counted by system
Definition: The platform applied the same contribution or leave deduction twice simultaneously due to a logic error in the calculation engine

Sub-Category Name: Statutory cap not enforced by system
Definition: The platform failed to stop contributions at the statutory annual or quarterly cap, resulting in an overstated charge that must be refunded

Sub-Category Name: Salary or allowance peg pulled incorrectly
Parent Category: Data or salary peg integrity error
Definition: The system used the wrong salary peg or exchange rate to calculate an employee's salary or allowance, producing a variance with the provider

Sub-Category Name: Employee incorrectly retained or reactivated in system
Parent Category: Data or salary peg integrity error
Definition: An employee who should have been terminated or deactivated was retained or reactivated in the system, causing incorrect ongoing invoicing

Sub-Category Name: Invoice generated for wrong company or period
Definition: The system invoiced the wrong company or incorrectly billed from a historical start date due to a company transfer or reactivation bug

Sub-Category Name: Net deduction or bonus overpaid due to system error
Definition: The system processed a net bonus or deduction twice, resulting in an overpayment that must be recovered via a manual adjustment

Sub-Category Name: Territory-specific tax variance under investigation
Definition: A general or complex tax variance in a specific territory where the cause has not yet been identified and active research is ongoing

Sub-Category Name: State or regional tax variance under investigation
Definition: A tax variance driven by state, provincial, or regional rules where further location-specific research is required

Sub-Category Name: Employee-specific tax treatment under investigation
Definition: A variance caused by an employee's individual tax status, exemption, or rate that requires investigation before a platform update can be made

Sub-Category Name: Benefit or allowance tax treatment under investigation
Definition: A variance related to how a benefit, allowance, or voucher is taxed where the correct treatment is disputed or unclear

Sub-Category Name: Statutory rate update logged but not yet live
Parent Category: Known rate or rule change not yet applied
Definition: A known change to a statutory contribution rate or tax rate has been identified and a platform update logged, but not yet applied

Sub-Category Name: Annual cap or threshold update not yet applied
Parent Category: Known rate or rule change not yet applied
Definition: An annual cap or contribution ceiling has changed and the platform has not yet been updated to reflect the new limit

Sub-Category Name: Year-start calculator update missed
Parent Category: Known rate or rule change not yet applied
Definition: The calculator was not updated at the start of the new tax year, resulting in outdated rates or rules being applied across the period

Sub-Category Name: Contribution base rule change identified
Parent Category: Known rate or rule change not yet applied
Definition: The basis on which a statutory contribution is calculated has changed or was found to be incorrect and an update has been initiated

Sub-Category Name: Statutory contribution not built into calculator
Definition: A mandatory employer contribution or levy is not programmed in the cost calculator and must be adjusted manually each month

Sub-Category Name: Employee-specific rate or exemption not modeled
Definition: An individual employee's tax rate, exemption, or contribution arrangement cannot be captured in the current calculator setup

Sub-Category Name: Complex formula-driven contribution not modeled
Definition: A statutory contribution that depends on a complex or variable calculation method that the calculator cannot currently replicate
